touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Riley Goldberg at CometEngine, a fintech company exploring revenue intelligence solutions. Riley expressed interest in improving pipeline visibility across their sales team. Currently using basic forecasting methods and evaluating Gong. Agreed to a qualification call to discuss current challenges and requirements.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently tracking pipeline visibility across your sales team?
questions_asked.answer: We're using a mix of spreadsheets and native CRM reports, but it's not giving us the real-time insights we need. Our forecasting accuracy has been inconsistent.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform differ from Gong? We've been looking at them as well.
questions_asked.answer: Great question—we focus heavily on automation and workflow integration, especially for teams your size. I'd love to walk through a comparison on our next call if that's helpful.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next week
Send overview deck highlighting pipeline visibility features

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Riley Goldberg to assess fit and current pain points. Riley shared that CometEngine's sales team struggles with forecast accuracy and lacks visibility into deal progression. They're a 34-person fintech with 8 sales reps. Currently evaluating Gong but concerned about cost and complexity for their team size. Riley identified pipeline visibility and automation as top priorities. Confirmed budget authority sits with COO Riley Adams.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We missed our quarterly forecast by 22% last quarter. Leadership needs better visibility and we need to move fast—probably making a decision within the next 4-6 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating and implementing a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Our COO Riley Adams would need to sign off on budget, and Hazel Davis in IT would handle the technical side. We'd also want our implementation lead Sarah Gupta involved.

questions_asked.question: What's the price range we're looking at for 8 seats?
questions_asked.answer: I'll put together a proposal, but for 8 seats with the automation features you need, we're typically in the $8-10K ACV range. Let me work up options for you.

objections: We need to make sure this isn't overly complex—Gong seems powerful but maybe too much for a team our size.
next_steps: Schedule discovery call to dive deeper into use cases
Introduce Riley Adams and Hazel Davis to the conversation
Prepare ROI analysis based on forecast accuracy improvement

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Riley Goldberg focusing on technical requirements and specific use cases. Discussed CometEngine's need for real-time pipeline tracking, deal health scoring, and automated follow-up workflows. Riley emphasized the importance of ease of implementation given their small IT team. Confirmed 8 seats needed (sales reps) with unlimited automation tier. Mentioned Gong pricing came in higher than expected and implementation timeline was a concern. Agreed to loop in COO and IT Director for technical validation and budget approval.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you 6 months after implementing this solution?
questions_asked.answer: Forecast accuracy within 10%, automated deal health alerts for at-risk opportunities, and our reps spending less time on admin and more time selling. We also want visibility for leadership without constant manual updates.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take? Gong quoted us 6-8 weeks and that feels long.
questions_asked.answer: For a team your size with our unlimited automation package, we typically see 2-3 weeks from kickoff to full deployment. We'll assign Sarah from your team as the implementation lead and our team handles most of the heavy lifting.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of next month. We're getting final pricing from Gong this week, so ideally we'd have your proposal soon to compare.

objections: We need to be confident the ROI justifies the investment—we're a small team and budget is tight this quarter.
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options for 8 seats, unlimited automation tier
Schedule technical review call with Hazel Davis (IT Director)
Provide case study of similar-sized fintech customer

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Riley Goldberg with formal proposal: 8 seats, unlimited automation tier, $5,300 ACV (41.7% discount from list to compete with Gong pricing). Proposal includes implementation timeline (3 weeks), ROI projection based on forecast accuracy improvement, and comparison matrix vs. Gong. Also attached case study from similar fintech customer. Riley responded same day confirming proposal received and will review with Riley Adams (COO) and Hazel Davis (IT) this week. Requested minor clarification on data retention policies.

questions_asked.question: Can you clarify the data retention policy on call recordings and email logs under the unlimited automation tier?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited retention for all data—no archiving limits. I've added that detail to the proposal attachment. Happy to walk through any other questions with your IT director.

next_steps: Riley to review proposal with COO and IT Director by end of week
Schedule follow-up call early next week to address questions
Prepare contract for signature pending internal approval

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from Riley Goldberg indicating internal team reviewed the proposal. COO Riley Adams approved budget at the $5,300 ACV price point. Hazel Davis (IT) raised questions about integration complexity with their existing CRM (Salesforce). Riley requested final terms confirmation and asked about payment structure options (annual vs. quarterly). Tone is positive but cautious on technical implementation scope. Rep responded with integration documentation and offered flexible payment terms (quarterly option available).

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments instead of annual upfront? It would help with our Q2 budget constraints.
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely—we can structure this as quarterly payments at $1,325 per quarter. I'll update the contract to reflect that and send it over today.

next_steps: Send updated contract with quarterly payment terms
Schedule technical onboarding call with Hazel Davis and Sarah Gupta
Target signature by end of week to begin implementation next month
